ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh6-126 name field. National Pokedex no. 676. Kalos poodle with 10 grooming-form variants.

trivia_bullets.text: Furfrou (National Pokedex no. 676, Generation VI, Kalos) can be trimmed into 10 distinct forms: Natural, Heart, Star, Diamond, Debutante, Matron, Dandy, La Reine, Kabuki, and Pharaoh Trim. In Pokemon X and Y, grooming was performed at Friseur Furfrou on Vernal Avenue in Lumiose City for $500; the trim reverts to Natural Form after five days or if deposited in a Box.

"Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it." The trust-gated grooming mechanic is built directly into the flavor: form-change requires a bond, not just a fee. This connects to the 'Find a Friend' attack name — both mechanics frame companionship as prerequisite.

Furfrou's 10-form grooming system is a strong forms-cycle node candidate analogous to Castform (which similarly has multiple visually distinct forms with non-evolution triggers). Unlike Castform (weather-triggered), Furfrou's form changes are player-initiated and cosmetic-only with no stat changes — aesthetic labor, not mechanical divergence.

Some Furfrou trims were geographically restricted in Pokemon GO: La Reine (France), Kabuki (Japan), and Pharaoh (Egypt) required region-specific Stardust costs to unlock, making Furfrou one of the few Pokemon with cosmetic forms tied to physical geography in the mobile game.
sources: Dexerto: Furfrou forms in Pokemon GO
